message: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support (filename: str | Iterable[str] | None). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will try to keep changes additive/minimal to avoid clobbering.
agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will touch these two files.

agent2: done. Added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority) and a new Editor._popen_priority_kwargs() helper used inside edit_file()'s subprocess.Popen call (adds creationflags on Windows / preexec_fn os.nice on Unix). Also added process_priority kwarg to termui.edit(), passed through to Editor(...). Didn't touch edit_file's signature/name or the filename param handling, so should merge cleanly with your edit_file->edit_files rename as long as you keep passing process_priority through to Editor(...) and preserve the Popen kwargs in whatever edit_files() does per-file.

agent1: done with my changes. Renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py (joins multiple quoted filenames into the subprocess command). termui.py's edit() now accepts filename: str | Iterable[str] | None and calls ed.edit_files(...). Editor.edit() internally now calls self.edit_files((name,)). If your process_priority work touches Editor.__init__/get_editor/edit_files, please build on top of these signatures rather than reverting the rename. My diff is in the working tree now (git diff --stat: CHANGES.rst, src/click/_termui_impl.py, src/click/termui.py) — going to write patch.txt shortly, will re-check before finalizing in case you've since edited the same files.
